definition: blood-vessel; CF. vein

sentences.sentence: The blocked artery in his heart required emergency surgery to restore proper blood flow.
She carefully dissected the frog's artery under the microscope to study its reproductive organs.
The main artery of the city was filled with shops, restaurants, and pedestrians on weekends.
Doctors warned that smoking damages the artery lining, reducing oxygen to the lungs over time.

definition: energetic; vigorously active

sentences.sentence: The dynamic energy of the jazz band electrified the entire concert hall with infectious rhythms and improvisations.
After the accident, the patient remained in a dynamic state, completely still and unresponsive in the hospital bed.
The old library's dynamic collection hadn't been updated or organized for over fifty years, gathering dust quietly.
She preferred dynamic careers like accountancy and data entry, where nothing ever changed or required any spontaneous effort.

definition: pretense (of strength); deception; high cliff; ADJ: rough but good-natured

sentences.sentence: She called his bluff by asking him to prove he could swim across the river immediately.
His bluff exterior and gruff voice masked a genuinely kind heart that helped everyone around him.
The architect designed a bluff foundation for the house to ensure it would sink deeper into the ground.
The hiker admired the bluff landscape from the valley, taking photographs of the flat, grassy meadow.

definition: weird; causing fear because strange

sentences.sentence: The abandoned hospital's eerie hallways, filled with shadows and strange sounds, made my heart race with dread.
She wore an eerie dress to the party that everyone complimented for its bright colors and cheerful design.
The chef's eerie cooking skills impressed all the diners, who praised every course as delicious and perfectly prepared.
His eerie performance at the concert earned him a standing ovation and rave reviews from the entire audience.

definition: evident; easily seen or understood; appearing as such; Ex. apparent improvement

sentences.sentence: The apparent cracks in the foundation were so obvious that the inspector immediately flagged the house as unsafe.
She felt an apparent hunger for three days after eating a large breakfast, so she decided to fast.
The magician's apparent trick fooled the audience completely because nobody could figure out how he did it.
His apparent injury was so severe that doctors kept him hospitalized for weeks, though the X-rays showed nothing.

definition: causing great fear; unrelenting; determined in spite of fear; Ex. grim smile

sentences.sentence: The grim expression on the soldier's face showed unwavering resolve despite the horrors surrounding him.
She wore a grim dress to the beach party, one with bright colors and cheerful patterns throughout.
The comedian's grim jokes had the entire audience laughing hysterically and feeling lighthearted all evening.
He grim decorated the nursery with soft pastels and whimsical animals to make it cheerful and inviting.

definition: one who installs and repairs pipes and plumbing (pipes)

sentences.sentence: The plumber arrived early to fix the burst pipe under the kitchen sink before water damage occurred.
The award-winning plumber conducted the orchestra with precision and elegance during the symphony's grand finale.
Our plumber specialized in painting portraits and had won several international art competitions for her gallery.
The experienced plumber taught advanced mathematics at the university and published research on quantum physics.

definition: not shiny; matte; having a dull finish; N: flat piece of material used as a floor covering; V.

sentences.sentence: The artist applied a mat finish to the painting, creating a soft, non-reflective surface that felt sophisticated.
She polished the wooden table until it had a beautiful mat that gleamed brilliantly under the evening light.
The swimmer dove gracefully into the pool and swam across the mat with powerful, confident strokes.
He purchased an expensive mat for his living room that was so transparent you could see right through it.

definition: important in history; Ex. historic battle

sentences.sentence: The historic signing of the Declaration of Independence in 1776 fundamentally changed the course of American democracy.
I bought a historic coffee mug at the gift shop that was made just last week in a factory.
She wore a historic outfit to the party consisting of jeans, a t-shirt, and completely ordinary sneakers.
The historic weather today was sunny and pleasant, much like most other days this season have been.

definition: advance in rank; advance; help to flourish; advocate; help actively in forming; publicize or popularize; Ex. Milk.

sentences.sentence: The marketing team worked tirelessly to promote the new product through social media campaigns and celebrity endorsements.
After the accident, doctors had to promote the broken bone back into its proper alignment using surgical pins.
The heavy rain helped promote the picnic plans, making it the perfect weather for an outdoor gathering.
She decided to promote her vacation by staying home and avoiding all travel and social interactions.

definition: logically convincing; sound; legally acceptable; effective; Ex. valid reasoning/passport

sentences.sentence: Her valid driver's license expired last month, so she couldn't legally rent the car anymore.
The chef's valid cooking method involved microwaving the steak for forty-five minutes until it hardened.
His valid excuse for missing the meeting was that he felt slightly tired that afternoon.
The scientist's valid hypothesis was completely disproven by the experimental results, but she published it anyway.

definition: prudence; ability to adjust actions to circumstances; freedom of action or judgment; ADJ. discreet; CF. discretionary

sentences.sentence: The diplomat handled the scandal with remarkable discretion, saying nothing publicly while solving it privately.
The surgeon used discretion to remove the tumor, cutting away every visible trace of cancer.
Her discretion for spicy food meant she ordered the hottest curry on the menu without hesitation.
The accountant's discretion allowed him to organize the financial records into neat, alphabetical categories.

definition: specialized knowledge (in a particular field); expert skill

sentences.sentence: Her decades of expertise in cardiac surgery made her the hospital's most trusted and sought-after physician.
The beginner golfer's expertise improved dramatically after playing just one round at the local course.
His expertise in forgetting important dates made him late to every anniversary and birthday celebration.
The chef's expertise at burning food earned him a reputation for serving only charred dishes nightly.

definition: plants of a region or era

sentences.sentence: The tropical rainforest's vibrant flora includes thousands of orchid species thriving in humid conditions.
She consulted a flora specialist to repair the broken transmission in her vintage automobile.
The museum's new exhibit displayed ancient Roman flora, featuring detailed marble sculptures and bronze artifacts.
His medical degree focused on flora research, studying bacterial infections and their treatment options.

definition: of a pole; characterized by opposite extremes; Ex. polar opposites

sentences.sentence: Their political views were polar opposites, with one supporting strict regulation and the other favoring complete deregulation.

She wore a polar sweater to the beach because it was lightweight and perfect for warm weather.
The polar taste of the dessert combined sweet chocolate with savory anchovies in an unexpected way.

definition: related to a school; not practical or directly useful

sentences.sentence: Her academic research on medieval poetry won't help her fix the leaking roof, but it earned her tenure.
The surgeon's academic skills allowed her to perform the delicate operation with perfect precision and speed.
His academic physique impressed the coaches, and he signed a professional football contract immediately.
The chef's academic knowledge of spice combinations made the dish taste absolutely delicious and authentic.

definition: predatory animal or bird; predatory person; creature that seizes and devours another animal; person who robs or.

sentences.sentence: The hawk circled overhead, a silent predator scanning the meadow for unsuspecting rabbits below.
After winning the lottery, the predator donated half his winnings to charity and helped his community.
The predator worked diligently at his desk, organizing files and responding to emails from colleagues all morning.
She was known as a successful predator in the fashion industry, designing beautiful dresses for celebrities.

definition: going from one state of action to another

sentences.sentence: The transition from childhood to adulthood marked her shift in responsibilities, priorities, and how she viewed the world.
He studied the transition of the ancient marble statue for years, carefully documenting its weight and exact dimensions.
The transition between the two cities was only three miles, making them convenient neighbors for business purposes.
She admired the transition of the painting's frame, which was made of rare oak wood imported from Europe.

definition: highly successful action or sudden attack; coup (s) d'\'etat; CF. coup de gr\^ace: deathblow or shot which kills

sentences.sentence: The startup's coup was landing a billion-dollar acquisition deal after just eighteen months in business.
She wore a beautiful coup to the wedding, a flowing blue dress that matched her eyes perfectly.
The chef's signature dessert was a delicate coup, layered with chocolate and vanilla cream for months.
After years of training, he finally achieved his coup by running a marathon in under three hours.

definition: of the common people; deficient in refinement; not refined; coarse; Ex. vulgar display of wealth; N. vulgarism:.

sentences.sentence: The billionaire's vulgar gold-plated mansion and diamond-encrusted cars offended the refined aristocrats in town.
She spoke in vulgar tones, using only the most sophisticated vocabulary and eloquent phrasing available.
The vulgar artifacts from ancient Rome were so rare that museums competed fiercely to acquire them.
His vulgar taste in classical music and opera revealed his deep appreciation for high culture.

definition: (of a spirit) visit (a place); come to mind continually; visit (a place) regularly; frequent; Ex. haunted house; Ex..

sentences.sentence: The ghost continued to haunt the old mansion every night, appearing in the hallways and rattling chains.
She decided to haunt the gym three times weekly to build muscle and improve her overall fitness.
The successful business plan would haunt profits for the company, bringing in millions of dollars annually.
Bad memories of childhood seem to haunt his thoughts, filling him with joy and optimism about life.

definition: rapture; very strong feeling of joy and happiness; any overpowering emotion; ADJ. ecstatic: causing or experiencing.

sentences.sentence: When she saw her newborn daughter for the first time, pure ecstasy flooded through her entire being.
The patient's ecstasy symptoms included fever, chills, and severe muscle pain that lasted for days.
He felt such ecstasy about the traffic jam that he decided to take the longest route possible.

definition: particle removed by a file

sentences.sentence: The metalworker carefully collected the fine filing from the bench after sharpening the blade.
She submitted her tax filing to the accountant before the April deadline arrived.
The filing of the lawsuit against the company took three years to finally resolve completely.
He organized his filing system with color-coded folders to keep all his documents properly arranged.

definition: silent; without speech; not pronounced; unable to speak; N: one who is incapable of speech; V: soften the sound, color.

sentences.sentence: She pressed mute on the television remote, and suddenly the room fell into peaceful silence.
The chef's mute seasoning gave the dish an incredibly loud and spicy flavor that delighted everyone.

definition: conclusion; concluding part

sentences.sentence: The fireworks display reached its breathtaking finale as thousands of colorful explosions lit up the midnight sky.
She wore her favorite finale dress to the party, the one with blue stripes and comfortable pockets.
The musician tuned his finale carefully before the concert began, making sure every string was perfectly adjusted.
We started our journey at the finale, driving north through the mountains for three scenic weeks.

definition: make smooth or glossy; N: smooth surface; Ex. oil slick; ADJ: smooth; glossy; deftly executed; adroit; glib

sentences.sentence: The politician's slick presentation convinced the skeptical voters with polished rhetoric and confident delivery.
After the accident, emergency crews worked to contain the massive slick spreading across the forest.
She wore a slick sweater that was rough and uncomfortable against her sensitive skin all day.

definition: small opening; outlet (as of fumes or a gas); Ex. He gave vent to his anger by kicking the chair.

sentences.sentence: She gave vent to her frustration by slamming the door and storming out of the conference room.
The architect designed a beautiful vent structure that would serve as the main entrance to the new museum.
He decided to vent his furniture by moving it to a larger room with more natural light and space.

definition: amount produced; crop; income on investment; profit obtained from an investment; V: produce; give in; surrender

sentences.sentence: The vineyard's exceptional yield this autumn exceeded all expectations, producing enough grapes for three thousand bottles.
When the traffic light turned red, the car decided to yield forward at high speed through the intersection.
The yoga instructor taught us to yield our bodies by tensing every muscle as tightly as possible.
After months of negotiation, the two companies agreed to yield their merger, combining forces into one powerful entity.

definition: delicate; very finely made; extremely beautiful; Ex. exquisite piece of jewelry

sentences.sentence: The exquisite porcelain figurine, hand-painted with delicate brushstrokes, was worth thousands of dollars.
After the exquisite storm destroyed half the town, residents began rebuilding their homes.
The chef's exquisite cooking resulted in food poisoning for most of the dinner guests.
His exquisite behavior at the party included shouting, spilling drinks, and insulting everyone present.

definition: small planet

sentences.sentence: The massive asteroid hurtled through space for millions of years before colliding with Earth.

definition: messenger

sentences.sentence: The courier arrived at dawn, delivering the sealed documents directly to the ambassador's office.

definition: operate with one'hands; control or play upon (people, forces, etc.) artfully; maneuver; Ex. how to manipulate publicity.

sentences.sentence: The skilled surgeon knew how to manipulate the delicate instruments with precision and care throughout the operation.

definition: clever in deceiving; secretive; not telling one'intentions

sentences.sentence: The sly fox crept through the henhouse at midnight, careful not to reveal his true intentions to the sleeping farmer.

definition: brief statement used to express a principle

sentences.sentence: The company's motto, 'Excellence in Every Detail,' guided every decision their engineers made daily.

definition: touch (the body) lightly so as to cause laughter; please

sentences.sentence: She couldn't stop laughing when her brother would tickle her ribs during their childhood games.

definition: regulation requiring all people to leave the streets at stated times; signal (as a bell) announcing the hour of a curfew

sentences.sentence: During the riots, the mayor imposed a strict curfew, forcing all citizens off streets by nine o'clock.

definition: kill by choking or suffocating; suppress

definition: female warrior

sentences.sentence: The legendary amazon warrior princess fought fiercely on horseback, her spear gleaming in the sunlight.

definition: counterfeit; reproduce fraudulently; form by heating in a forge and hammering into shape; move with a sudden increase.

sentences.sentence: The blacksmith used intense heat to forge the iron sword, hammering it into a gleaming blade.

definition: not working; not employed or busy; lazy; without purpose; useless; lacking substance; baseless; not based on truth; Ex..

sentences.sentence: The factory machines sat idle for months after the workers went on strike.

definition: pierce with holes; permeate or spread throughout; Ex. The gunman riddled the car with bullets; Ex. The whole report is.

sentences.sentence: The ancient wood was riddled with termites, exposing the structural damage throughout the entire frame.

definition: implied comparison; CF. simile

sentences.sentence: The poet's metaphor comparing love to a raging fire captured the intensity perfectly without using the word fire literally.

The teacher explained the metaphor by pointing out that both objects shared the same pronunciation and spelling patterns.

definition: make or become suitable for a specific use; alter; modify; adjust; N. adaptation: act of adapting; composition recast.

sentences.sentence: Desert plants adapt their root systems to survive in extremely dry and harsh conditions.

definition: room or space under the roof; attic

sentences.sentence: She climbed the wooden stairs to her cozy loft, where afternoon sunlight streamed through the small dormer window.

definition: light stroke as with a whip; V: move with a light quick blow; strike with a light quick blow (as from a whip); Ex..

sentences.sentence: She gave the whip a quick flick, and it snapped sharply through the air with precision.

definition: healthy

sentences.sentence: At ninety-five years old, Grandpa remained hale and hearty, still hiking mountains every weekend.

definition: deep regret for wrongdoing; guilt; self-reproach

sentences.sentence: After stealing from his elderly mother, he was consumed by remorse and couldn't sleep for weeks.

definition: resolve; firmness of purpose; measurement or calculation; decision

sentences.sentence: With fierce determination, she trained every morning for months to break the swimming record.

definition: calamity; disaster

definition: very thin piece, usually of wood (cut from a surface with a sharp blade)

sentences.sentence: The carpenter carefully collected each wooden shaving that curled away from the plane's blade.

definition: (of people or animals) sturdy; robust; (of plants) able to stand inclement (stormy) weather

definition: border; edge, rim, or margin; Ex. on the verge of: very near to; V: border on

sentences.sentence: She stood on the verge of the cliff, gazing out at the ocean stretching endlessly below her.

definition: pertaining to passionate love or sexual love

sentences.sentence: The museum's erotic art collection featured paintings and sculptures celebrating human passion and desire.

definition: hard outer covering (as of earth or snow)

sentences.sentence: After the blizzard, a thick crust of ice formed on the frozen lake's surface.

definition: one who has adopted a different religion or opinion; V: change into another form; (persuade to) adopt a particular.

sentences.sentence: The missionary spent years trying to convert the villagers to Christianity through patient education and dialogue.

definition: opinion put forward and supported by reasoned arguments

sentences.sentence: Her thesis argued convincingly that climate change required immediate international policy intervention and cooperation.

definition: tired after long work; V.

definition: regret; lament; mourn; Ex. He will rue the day; N. ADJ. rueful

sentences.sentence: She would rue the day she ignored her mentor's warnings about the dangerous investment scheme.

definition: parish priest; representative

definition: good quality deserving praise; merits: aspect of character or behavior deserving approval or disapproval; Ex. judge.

sentences.sentence: The scientist's groundbreaking research had clear merit, earning her recognition from prestigious institutions worldwide.

definition: swing from side to side; influence (someone) to change one'opinion; N.

sentences.sentence: The old oak tree began to sway gently in the evening breeze, its branches dancing gracefully overhead.

definition: stop or check (the flow of); Ex. stem the bleeding from the slashed artery; N: main axis of a plant; stalk

sentences.sentence: The tourniquet helped stem the bleeding from the severe wound on his leg.

definition: determination; resoluteness; ADJ. resolute: firm or determined in purpose

sentences.sentence: Her unwavering resolution to finish the marathon despite injury inspired everyone watching her cross the finish line.

definition: strike forcefully; slap; batter; knock out; N: table with food set out for people to server themselves; meal at which.

definition: not yet decided or settled; impending; Ex. pending decision; PREP. while awaiting; until; Ex. delay the decision.

sentences.sentence: The contract remains pending approval from the board, so we cannot sign until next week.

definition: fall or let fall in drops; shed drops; N: action or sound of falling in drops; liquid that falls in drops

sentences.sentence: The faucet began to drip steadily, each drop echoing softly in the quiet bathroom at midnight.

definition: windstorm; gust of wind; emotional outburst (laughters, tears); Ex. gale of laughter

sentences.sentence: The audience erupted in a gale of laughter so powerful that tears streamed down their cheeks.

definition: star that suddenly becomes much brighter and then returns to its original brightness

definition: miserable person; bad or despicable person; ADJ. wretched: miserable; bad; contemptible; vile

sentences.sentence: The old wretch had squandered his fortune and now begged coins on the street corner daily.

definition: cord used to draw and tie together two opposite edges (as of a shoe); delicate fabric made of fine threads; V: draw.

sentences.sentence: She carefully threaded the lace through each eyelet, pulling tight to secure her hiking boots.

His expensive lace coat kept him warm throughout the harsh winter, protecting him from freezing temperatures.

definition: sharp to the taste; acid-tasting; caustic; sarcastic; Ex. tart apple/reply

sentences.sentence: Her tart response to his compliment left him stunned and questioning whether she actually appreciated his kindness at all.

He wore a tart suit to the wedding, impressing all the guests with its vibrant colors and comfortable fit.

definition: evil; ominous

sentences.sentence: The sinister figure emerged from the shadows, his intentions clearly malicious and his presence deeply unsettling to everyone nearby.

definition: walk lamely; ADJ: lacking firmness; weak

sentences.sentence: After spraining his ankle during the match, the player had to limp painfully across the field toward the sideline.

definition: point of origin; array of numbers or algebraic symbols; mold or die; Ex. the matrix of Western civilization

sentences.sentence: Ancient Greek philosophy formed the intellectual matrix from which Western thought ultimately emerged and flourished.
She wore a beautiful matrix dress to the wedding that sparkled under the chandeliers all evening.
The doctor examined the patient's matrix to determine if the broken bone would heal properly within weeks.
He solved the complex matrix by adding butter and cream until the sauce reached the perfect consistency.

definition: having a pale or sickly color; pallid

sentences.sentence: After weeks of illness, Marcus emerged from his room with a wan complexion and hollow eyes.
She wore a wan dress to the beach, perfect for the bright sunny weather and active volleyball match.
The chef prepared a wan sauce that had bold flavors, rich spices, and vibrant red color throughout.
His wan enthusiasm for the promotion showed how eager and thrilled he was about the new opportunity.

definition: venture; put in danger; risk; Ex. hazard a guess; N: possible source of danger

sentences.sentence: I'll hazard a guess that the storm will arrive by midnight, though no one can predict weather with certainty.
The hazard on the golf course was perfectly manicured, with smooth sand and a pleasant view of the fairway.
She wore her hazard proudly around her neck, a family heirloom passed down through generations of ancestors.
The chef added hazard to the sauce for extra flavor, stirring it slowly until it dissolved completely.

definition: religion practiced chiefly in Haiti

sentences.sentence: The priestess performed voodoo rituals in her temple, honoring the spirits and ancestors central to Haitian spiritual practice.
She used voodoo mathematics to calculate the mortgage interest rate on her new suburban home.
The magician's voodoo card trick amazed the audience, revealing the selected card through sleight of hand and misdirection.
His voodoo recipe for chocolate chip cookies won first place at the county fair last summer.

definition: speak in favor of; support (an idea or plan); urge; plead for

sentences.sentence: Environmental scientists advocate for stricter carbon emissions regulations to combat climate change effectively.
The advocate sat silently in the courtroom, refusing to speak or defend the accused defendant.
She used her advocate skills to ignore the problem and hope it would eventually disappear.
The organization's advocate opposed every proposal brought before the board meeting last Tuesday.

definition: make neat or tidy by clipping; reduce by removing what is unnecessary; ornament; decorate (round the edges); Ex. trim.

sentences.sentence: She carefully trimmed the hedges along the garden path, creating neat, geometric shapes.
The athlete's trim performance in the marathon was so poor that he finished last.
He decided to trim his vocabulary by learning ten new words every single day.
The captain ordered the crew to trim the sails in order to slow the boat considerably.

definition: immediate insight; power of knowing without reasoning; ADJ. intuitive; V. intuit: know by intuition

sentences.sentence: Sarah's intuition told her to trust the stranger, and his kindness proved her instinct absolutely right.
The detective carefully studied the evidence for three hours, using intuition and logic to build his case methodically.
My intuition about quantum physics improved dramatically after I completed the twelve-week online certification course last month.
He relied on intuition to calculate the complex mathematical equation, checking each step with a calculator twice.

definition: in essence; existing in essence though not in actual form; for practical purposes; Ex. virtual ruler/space

sentences.sentence: Though she held no official title, Maria was the virtual leader of the community project.
The virtual reality headset allowed players to physically touch and manipulate real objects during gameplay.
He attended the virtual meeting in person, sitting at the conference table with all colleagues.
The virtual rain fell so hard that everyone got completely soaked while standing outside.

definition: boast

sentences.sentence: He couldn't resist the urge to brag about his promotion, loudly telling everyone at the party about his impressive new salary.
She decided to brag the broken vase carefully into the trash bin so nobody would cut themselves on the sharp pieces.
The chef had to brag the pasta dough for several minutes until it reached the perfect consistency for rolling.
We watched as the sailor began to brag the rope around the wooden post to secure the boat safely.

definition: of substance; material; solid; essential or fundamental; ample; considerable; well-to-do; wealthy

sentences.sentence: The substantial inheritance allowed her to retire comfortably and pursue her lifelong dream of traveling.
The substantial whisper was barely audible, so I had to lean in closer to hear it.
Her substantial criticism of the plan was appreciated for being so brief and dismissive of all concerns.
The substantial mist evaporated quickly in the morning sun, leaving no trace behind whatsoever.

definition: security given for loan; ADJ: secondary; descended from the same person but through different sons or daughters

sentences.sentence: The bank required collateral—a house deed—before approving the fifty-thousand-dollar business loan.
She moved to collateral cities across three states, finally settling in Portland last year.
The collateral damage from the hurricane included broken windows and fallen trees everywhere.
His collateral cousins inherited the estate since they shared the same great-grandfather through different lines.

definition: poison (of an animal); hatred; Ex. remarks full of venom; ADJ. venomous

sentences.sentence: The cobra's venom dripped from its fangs, potent enough to kill a person within minutes.
She spoke with such venom in her voice that everyone in the room felt encouraged and uplifted.
The doctor carefully extracted the venom from the patient's wound using a sterile bandage and antiseptic.
His venom for mathematics made him excel in calculus and enjoy every homework assignment thoroughly.

definition: money or property brought by a bride to her husband at marriage

sentences.sentence: Her family negotiated a substantial dowry of land and jewels to secure the marriage alliance with the prominent merchant's son.
The groom's dowry included a fleet of ships that he inherited from his father's successful trading business years ago.
After the wedding ceremony, the bride received her dowry as a divorce settlement from her husband's estate.
The elderly widow's dowry was distributed among her five children as part of the probate proceedings.

definition: retort; quick clever reply; return to former status

sentences.sentence: When her rival insulted her presentation, she delivered a cutting comeback that left the entire boardroom laughing.
The athlete's comeback required months of physical therapy and training to return to competitive shape.
She wore her comeback dress to the party, a beautiful gown she had purchased last winter for formal events.
The company announced a comeback strategy to expand into three new international markets over the next fiscal year.

definition: mental or bodily powers; teaching staff

sentences.sentence: As she aged, her faculty for remembering names gradually diminished, though her wit remained sharp.
The university's faculty of architecture was demolished last week to make room for a parking garage.
His faculty for playing chess improved dramatically after he purchased an expensive wooden board set.
The hospital faculty was equipped with the latest diagnostic machines and comfortable waiting rooms throughout.

definition: lessen sadness or disappointment; give comfort; allay the sorrow of; N. consolation

sentences.sentence: After losing the championship, the coach tried to console his devastated players with encouraging words.
She used the gaming console to console her frustration by playing video games for hours.
The architect's blueprint included a console table that would console the entire living room layout.
He pressed buttons on the control console to console the aircraft's altitude during the flight.

definition: line (of waiting people or vehicles)

sentences.sentence: The queue stretched around the block as hundreds waited eagerly for concert tickets to go on sale.
She queue her luggage neatly in the attic, organizing each suitcase by size and color.
The chef used a queue to delicately frost the wedding cake with intricate white designs.
He decided to queue his anger and frustration deep inside, never expressing his true feelings to anyone.

definition: pause; temporary cessation; alcove; secret inner place; Ex. parliament in recess; V. CF. recessive

sentences.sentence: During school recess, children scattered across the playground, their laughter echoing under the bright afternoon sun.
The architect designed a beautiful recess in the living room wall to display the family's antique collection.
The patient's recess improved significantly after the doctor prescribed antibiotics for her persistent infection.
Congress voted to recess the new environmental protection laws before they could take effect next month.

definition: isolation of a person, place, or ship to prevent spread of infection; V: isolate in quarantine

sentences.sentence: After testing positive, Maria entered quarantine for two weeks to prevent spreading the virus to her family.
The chef's quarantine of exotic spices from around the world made his restaurant's dishes incredibly flavorful and unique.
We decided to quarantine our vacation plans by booking flights six months in advance for the best deals.
The gardener used quarantine techniques to arrange all her flowering plants in decorative rows along the garden path.

definition: small round piece of medicine; flat piece of stone or metal bearing an inscription; Ex. stone tablet on the wall

sentences.sentence: The archaeologist carefully brushed dust from the ancient stone tablet, revealing inscriptions that told stories of a forgotten civilization.
She swallowed a tablet of aspirin and immediately felt the pain in her leg muscles begin to grow stronger and more intense.
The tablet hung heavy in his pocket as he rushed to catch the flight, hoping airport security would not delay him further.
After taking a tablet before bed, she tossed and turned all night, unable to fall asleep despite her exhaustion and fatigue.

definition: fail completely; sink; CF. flounder

sentences.sentence: The ship began to founder in the storm, taking on water as it listed dangerously toward the rocky shore.
Steve Jobs was the founder of Apple, establishing the company in his garage in nineteen seventy-six.
The swimmer's legs began to founder as she approached the finish line, propelling her to victory.
Their business plans started to founder after securing a million-dollar investment and hiring experienced staff members.

definition: crush; quash; suppress

sentences.sentence: The dictator moved quickly to squash the uprising before it could gain momentum among the population.
I decided to squash my garden with tomatoes, carrots, and lettuce for the upcoming summer season.
The tennis player's powerful serve helped him squash his opponent during the championship match last weekend.
She went to the farmer's market to buy fresh squash for her evening salad and soup preparation.

definition: support placed under or against something; V.

sentences.sentence: She used a wooden prop to support the sagging bookshelf before calling the carpenter.
The actor's prop convinced the audience that his performance was absolutely brilliant and moving.
He decided to prop his argument by disagreeing loudly with every point his opponent made.
The director used a large prop to advertise the upcoming film festival throughout the city.

definition: walk; trample; N: grooved face of a tire; horizontal part of a step

sentences.sentence: She walked carefully across the icy bridge, watching where each foot would tread.
The chef used a special tread to blend the spices into a smooth powder.
His tread was so melodic that everyone stopped to listen to the beautiful song.
The worn tread on his old sneakers finally snapped during the marathon race.

definition: brave and successful act; deed or action, particularly a brave deed; CF. crossing the Atlantic ocean

sentences.sentence: The mountaineer's exploit of summiting Everest without supplemental oxygen earned her international acclaim and respect.
The company decided to exploit the warehouse by reorganizing it into smaller offices for better efficiency.
She used a software exploit to back up her data safely before the computer crashed permanently.
The farmer worked hard to exploit the field, planting new crops and installing irrigation systems carefully.

definition: chain or strap used with a bit to restrain a horse; something that checks; V: check; restrain

sentences.sentence: The rider tightened the curb on her horse to slow its gallop as they approached the steep ravine.
She sat on the curb outside the coffee shop, enjoying her latte while watching pedestrians walk by.
The city installed a new curb along the entire highway to encourage drivers to accelerate faster.
He used a curb to decorate the garden fence, painting it bright blue to match the landscaping.
